preview_text:
The model favors Switzerland heavily, assigning an 81% win probability against a Qatar side credited with only a 3% chance of victory. The gap reflects a decisive expected quality advantage, leaving the underdog minimal paths to success.

The win-draw-loss spread widens decisively toward the favorite. Switzerland's 81% win probability substantially outpaces the 16% draw chance, indicating the model expects a competitive match structure but one tilted sharply toward a Swiss victory. A Qatar win remains a low-probability tail outcome in the model's view.

The model and sportsbooks align closely on the headline: Switzerland's win probability sits 2 percentage points higher in the model than across the 48-book consensus. Both sources treat a draw as unlikely relative to a Swiss win, though bookmakers rate the draw slightly lower. Polymarket prices track the sportsbook consensus tightly. The minor divergences suggest broad agreement on the match's likely shape.
